text_blocks.content:
Interim Report – Document Intelligence Refinery
1. Domain Notes (Phase 0 Deliverable)
1.1 Problem Context
Modern Financial Data Engineering (FDE) work must ingest heterogeneous
documents (native PDFs, scanned legal documents, technical reports, and table-heavy fiscal
summaries) and turn them into auditable, queryable knowledge. The primary risks are:
 Garbage-in → hallucination-out: weak extraction corrupts downstream RAG.
 Layout blindness: multi-column and table-heavy layouts often collapse into flat text.
 Provenance blindness: systems answer confidently without source trace.
This refinery is designed as a 5-stage, agentic pipeline that explicitly separates:
1. Document profiling (Triage)
2. Structure extraction with multi-strategy routing (A/B/C)
3. Semantic chunking into Logical Document Units (LDUs)
4. Page-level navigation structure (PageIndex)
5. Query + provenance / audit layer
The goal is to handle Classes A–D robustly, with explicit escalation from cheap to expensive
strategies and cost-aware controls.
1.2 Extraction Strategy Decision Tree
Signals computed per document (from PDF parser / heuristics):
 Per-page character count.
 Per-page character density chars/page_areachars/page_area.
 Image ratio
proxy image_count/max_images_for_ratioimage_count/max_images_for_ratio.

 Line statistics: presence of pipe (|) characters, short-line frequency.
 Keyword signals for domain hints (financial / legal / technical / medical / general).
Step 1 – Origin Type Classification
 If avg_char_density < low_density_threshold and avg_image_ratio ≥
image_heavy_threshold
→ origin_type = scanned_image
 If avg_char_density ≥ high_density_threshold and avg_image_ratio < 0.3
→ origin_type = native_digital
 Else
→ origin_type = mixedStep 2 – Layout Complexity ClassificationCompute over concatenated
page text:
 Count pipe-like lines (contains | and ≥ 3 segments).
 Count short lines (length between ~8 and 32 chars).
 Look for figure keywords (“figure”, “chart”).
Heuristic:
 If pipe_like > 5 → layout_complexity = table_heavy
 Else if many_short_lines > max(0.45 * total_lines, 5) → layout_complexity = multi_column
 Else if “figure” or “chart” present → layout_complexity = figure_heavy
 Else → layout_complexity = single_column
Step 3 – Domain Hint
 For each domain, sum keyword counts in lowercased text:
 financial: “revenue”, “balance sheet”, “income statement”, “fiscal”, …
 legal: “clause”, “agreement”, “hereby”, …
 technical: “architecture”, “protocol”, “specification”, …
text_blocks.section_hint:  Line statistics: presence of pipe (|) characters, short-line frequency.

text_blocks.content:
 medical: “patient”, “diagnosis”, “clinical”, …
 Choose domain with highest score; default general.
Step 4 – Estimated Extraction Cost TierMapping:
 If origin_type = scanned_image
→ estimated_extraction_cost = needs_vision_model
 Else if layout_complexity ∈ {table_heavy, multi_column, mixed}
→ estimated_extraction_cost = needs_layout_model
 Else
→ estimated_extraction_cost = fast_text_sufficientStep 5 – Strategy Routing (A/B/C)Initial
strategy selection based on estimated_extraction_cost:
 fast_text_sufficient → Strategy A – FastTextExtractor
 needs_layout_model → Strategy B – LayoutExtractor
 needs_vision_model → Strategy C – VisionExtractor
Confidence-gated escalation:
1. Run chosen strategy → produce (ExtractedDocument, confidence_score, cost_estimate).
2. If confidence_score < confidence_minimum and strategy is A
→ escalate to B, recompute.
3. If still confidence_score < confidence_minimum and strategy is A or B
→ escalate to C, recompute.
4. If cost_estimate > budget_per_document_usd
→ fail fast with a budget guard error (no silent overruns).
1.3 Failure Modes Observed & Mitigations
1. Structure Collapse (tables flatten into paragraphs)

 Symptoms: Pipe- or grid-like tables become linear text, columns merge, numeric columns lose
alignment.
 Impact: Querying numeric facts or doing aggregates becomes unreliable; RAG misses
table context.
 Mitigation:
 Detect pipe-delimited table lines in fast-text extraction.
 Promote suspected tables to explicit TableObject with headers and rows.
 In layout-aware strategy, add heuristic promotions when text mentions “balance sheet”,
“income statement”, “table”, etc.
 In chunking, enforce “table cells are never split from headers”.
2. Context Poverty (chunks are too small or mis-grouped)
 Symptoms: Answers lose nearby context; cross-references like “see Table 3”
become meaningless.
 Impact: Embedding search operates on fragments that don’t carry enough semantics;
retrieval precision drops.
 Mitigation:
 Chunk at Logical Document Unit (LDU) level:
 Group paragraphs under section hints.
 Keep numbered lists as single LDUs when below max_tokens.
 Propagate section headers as parent metadata to all child chunks.
 Resolve cross-references into related_chunk_hashes linking LDUs that mention “Table N”.
3. Provenance Blindness (no trustworthy citation)
 Symptoms: System can answer but cannot show exactly which page/region supported
the claim.
 Impact: No auditability, high regulatory/compliance risk.
 Mitigation:
text_blocks.section_hint:  Symptoms: Pipe- or grid-like tables become linear text, columns merge, numeric

text_blocks.content:
 Every LDU carries:
 page_refs → (document_name, page_number, bbox)
 content_hash → stable spatial+content hash.
 ProvenanceChain is attached to every QueryAnswer:
 List of ProvenanceItem with document_name, page_number, bbox, content_hash.
4. Cost Overrun from Overuse of Vision / Layout Models
 Symptoms: All documents routed into expensive vision or layout strategies;
token/spend explodes.
 Impact: Unbounded costs at scale.
 Mitigation:
 Explicit estimated_extraction_cost tier and routing.
 budget_per_document_usd configured in YAML.
 Router tracks and enforces cost per document.

2. Architecture Diagram & 5-Stage Pipeline
This section describes the full 5-stage pipeline and its data contracts.
2.1 High-Level Architectural Diagram (Mermaid)
2.2 Stage 1 – Triage Agent (Document Classifier)
Input: Raw PDF path Output: DocumentProfile (saved
under .refinery/profiles/{doc_id}.json)Responsibility:
 Inspect per-page character density, image ratio, layout heuristics, and keywords.
 Classify:
 origin_type: native_digital | scanned_image | mixed | form_fillable
 layout_complexity: single_column | multi_column | table_heavy | figure_heavy | mixed
 language_code + language_confidence (currently default “en”, confidence ~0.85).
 domain_hint: financial | legal | technical | medical | general
 estimated_extraction_cost: fast_text_sufficient | needs_layout_model | needs_vision_model
 avg_char_density, avg_image_ratio.

This profile is the contract that governs routing in Stage 2.
2.3 Stage 2 – Multi-Strategy Extraction Layer
Input: document_path, DocumentProfileOutput: ExtractedDocument + ledger entryThree strategies:
 Strategy A — FastTextExtractor
 Use pypdf to extract page text and approximate page bounding box.
 Detect simple pipe-delimited tables; create TableObjects.
 Compute confidence:
 Average characters per page vs target_chars_per_page.
 Average density vs target_density.
 Image ratio penalty.
 Return ExtractedDocument with text_blocks, tables, figures.
 Strategy B — LayoutExtractor
 Subclass of FastText.
 In addition to A:
 Promote more tables when content contains financial/table keywords.
 Slightly increase confidence when tables are detected.
 Strategy C — VisionExtractor
 Subclass of FastText, but designed to handle scanned / image-heavy PDFs.
 Behavior:
 If fast text already found usable text, reuse it and re-label as vision_augmented.
 If no usable text:
 Call _ocr_pages_with_vision (currently placeholder) to produce page-level TextBlocks.
text_blocks.section_hint: This profile is the contract that governs routing in Stage 2.

text_blocks.content:
 Later: plug in LM Studio VLM/OCR here (render PDF pages to images, send to model,
parse text).
 Returns ExtractedDocument with text derived from OCR/VLM.
Router behavior:
 Picks A/B/C based on DocumentProfile.estimated_extraction_cost.
 Applies the confidence gate and escalation A→B→C.
 Enforces budget per document, raising if estimated cost exceeds configured cap.
 Logs to .refinery/extraction_ledger.jsonl via an ExtractionLedgerEntry.
2.4 Stage 3 – Semantic Chunking Engine (LDUs)
Input: ExtractedDocumentOutput: List[LDU] (Logical Document Units)Responsibilities:
 Convert text_blocks, tables, figures into structurally-aware LDUs.
 Enforce the 5 chunking rules (“Constitution”):
1. Table cell is never split from header row
 Whole table (header + rows) is wrapped into a single LDU of chunk_type="table".
2. Figure caption is stored as figure metadata
 Each figure chunk carries metadata["caption"].
3. Numbered list kept as single LDU unless exceeding max_tokens
 Detected via line patterns like 1. / 2) at line starts.
4. Section headers propagated as parent metadata
 parent_section and metadata["section_hint"] carry the section context.
5. Cross-references resolved into relationships
 For occurrences of “Table N” / “see Table N”, related_chunk_hashes link to table LDUs.
Each LDU includes:
text_blocks.section_hint:  Later: plug in LM Studio VLM/OCR here (render PDF pages to images, send to mod

text_blocks.content:
 content: chunk text (paragraph, list, table flattening, or caption).
 chunk_type: "text" | "table" | "figure" | "list".
 page_refs: list of (document_name, page_number, bbox).
 bounding_box: primary bbox for the chunk.
 parent_section: best-known section name or hint.
 token_count: approximate word-token count.
 content_hash: stable hash over (page, bbox, content).
 related_chunk_hashes, metadata: cross-links and extra annotations.
2.5 Stage 4 – PageIndex Builder & Storage
Input: LDUsOutputs:
 PageIndexNode tree saved under .refinery/pageindex/{doc_id}.json.
 Chunks ingested into:
 SimpleVectorStore (in-memory vector space).
 FactTableStore (SQLite facts.db).
PageIndexNode fields:
 title
 page_start, page_end
 child_sections: List[PageIndexNode]
 key_entities: List[str]
 summary: str
 data_types_present: List[str] (e.g. ["text", "table"])
Algorithm (heuristic baseline):
 Group LDUs by parent_section (or “Uncategorized”).
text_blocks.section_hint:  content: chunk text (paragraph, list, table flattening, or caption).

text_blocks.content:
 For each section:
 Determine page range from its LDUs.
 Extract simple key entities: capitalized tokens that look like names or key terms.
 Build a short summary from first N words of section content.
 Build a top-level “Document” node with child sections.
Additionally:
 Vector store: uses TF/cosine similarity over LDU content for semantic search.
 Fact table: regex-based extraction of key: value pairs into SQLite, keyed by page and content
hash.
2.6 Stage 5 – Query Interface Agent & Audit
Input:
 PageIndexNode tree
 LDUs (already ingested into vector and fact stores)
 Natural language question or claim
Tools exposed:
1. pageindex_navigate(topic, k)
 Scores child sections by match to topic in title and summary.
2. semantic_search(query, top_k, allowed_pages)
 Vector search restricted to allowed page ranges.
3. structured_query(sql)
 Direct SQL over facts.db.
Answer generation:
 Navigate PageIndex with question string to get top-K relevant sections.

 Restrict semantic search to pages in these sections.
 Collect top chunks; synthesize an answer (extractive + concatenative baseline).
 Build ProvenanceChain from the chunks:
 For each hit: document_name, page_number, bbox, content_hash.
Audit mode:
 Given a claim:
 Reuse the same pipeline to attempt retrieval.
 If ProvenanceChain.citations non-empty → claim considered “verified (has source)”.
 Else → “unverifiable / not found”.
3. Cost Analysis – Strategy Tiers
This section provides a concrete, per-document cost estimate for each strategy tier, assuming:
 Local PDF parsing and chunking are negligible vs. model calls.
 The main variable cost is tokens processed by external models (layout model or
VLM/OCR).
 We assume a hypothetical local LM Studio model or low-cost API; you can adapt the
numbers to your real pricing.
3.1 Assumptions
Let:
 PP = number of pages in the document.
 Typical page has:
 Strategy A (fast text): ~0.5–1k tokens (if later summarized/embedded).
 Strategy B (layout): ~1–1.5k tokens per page when using a layout-aware model.
 Strategy C (vision): ~1.5–2.0k tokens per page due to image+prompt overhead.
text_blocks.section_hint:  Restrict semantic search to pages in these sections.

text_blocks.content:
Token pricing assumptions (illustrative):
 Text model (for layout-level extraction / summarization):
 $0.25 per 1M tokens (input + output) – think cheap, local/hosted model.
 Vision model (for scanned PDFs):
 Effective cost equivalent of $1.00 per 1M tokens (since images are heavier).
We also set:
 Budget guard: budget_per_document_usd = 0.20 (configurable).
3.2 Strategy A – Fast Text (Low Cost)
Mechanics:
 Uses local pypdf text extraction only.
 No external model tokens required for extraction.
 Optional light summarization or embedding can be done with a very cheap local model.
Cost estimate per document:
 Extraction: ~$0.00 (local only).
 Optional summarization/embedding on 20 pages (P=20P=20), ~500 tokens per page:
 Total tokens: 20×500=10,00020×500=10,000 tokens.
 Cost: 10,000/1,000,000×$0.25≈$0.002510,000/1,000,000×$0.25≈$0.0025.
Range:
 \$0.00 – \$0.01 per document for typical 20–50 page native PDFs.
3.3 Strategy B – Layout-Aware (Medium Cost)
Mechanics:
 May invoke a layout-aware text model (Docling/MinerU-like behavior when later integrated).
 We assume per-page extraction prompt that includes layout hints and table expectations.

Cost estimate per document (P pages):
 Tokens per page: ~1,000–1,500.
 For P=30P=30:
 Total tokens: ~30–45k.
 At $0.25 / 1M tokens →
cost ≈ 45,000/1,000,000×$0.25=$0.0112545,000/1,000,000×$0.25=$0.01125
.
Add some headroom (retry / longer prompts):
 Typical range:
 \$0.01 – \$0.05 per 30–100 page technical or financial report.
This keeps most layout-heavy documents well under the \$0.20 per-doc budget, even with
multiple passes.
3.4 Strategy C – Vision-Augmented (High Cost)
Mechanics:
 For scanned PDFs and low-confidence A/B outputs, use a vision model via LM Studio:
 Convert each page to an image.
 Send image + structured extraction prompt to vision endpoint.
 Receive OCR text + structured hints.
Cost estimate per document (P pages):
 Assume 1.5k–2.0k tokens per page (image prompt + response).
 For P=20P=20:
 Tokens: 30–40k.
 At $1.00 / 1M tokens:
 Cost: 40,000/1,000,000×$1.00=$0.0440,000/1,000,000×$1.00=$0.04.
 For P=50P=50:

 Tokens: 75–100k.
 Cost: 0.075–0.100.075–0.10 USD.
Range:
 \$0.03 – \$0.15 per document for 20–50 page scanned PDFs, depending on page count and
retries.
Given the configured budget guard at \$0.20, this ensures:
 Even worst-case 50-page scanned documents are usually under budget.
 If a pathological case would exceed budget (e.g., repeated retries, very high page count),
the pipeline fails fast instead of silently overspending.
3.5 Strategy Mix Across Corpus Classes
For the target corpus:
 Class A – Annual Report (native, multi-column)
 Route: A (fast text) or B (layout-aware) for complex tables/columns.
 Expected tier: Mostly Strategy B.
 Cost range: \$0.01–\$0.05 per document.
 Class B – Scanned Government/Legal
 Route: C (vision-augmented) after triage & low-density detection.
 Expected tier: Strategy C.
 Cost range: \$0.05–\$0.15 per document.
 Class C – Technical Assessment (mixed)
 Route: Starts with B; escalates to C only if confidence is low for some pages.
 Expected tier: Mostly Strategy B, occasional C for scanned pages.
 Cost range: \$0.02–\$0.08 per document.
 Class D – Structured Data Report (table-heavy fiscal)

 Route: Primarily B for table-quality extraction.
 Expected tier: Strategy B with precise table handling.
 Cost range: \$0.02–\$0.06 per document.
These estimates demonstrate that most documents remain under \$0.10, and all are bounded
by the \$0.20 budget guard, while still allowing reliable escalation to layout and
vision strategies when needed.
4. Summary
This report describes:
 The domain-specific extraction strategy decision tree and observed failure modes
with mitigations.
 A 5-stage agentic architecture (Triage → Multi-Strategy Extraction →
Semantic Chunking → PageIndex & Storage → Query & Audit), with typed contracts
between stages.
 A confidence-gated, budget-aware routing design that escalates intelligently from cheap to
expensive strategies.
 A cost model for Strategy A/B/C, with per-document estimates aligned to the
configured budget guard and to realistic page counts for Classes A–D.
text_blocks.section_hint:  Route: Primarily B for table-quality extraction.
